goal: Add malformed source adapter smoke fixtures for Sherlog

summary.title: Add source adapter format-drift smoke tests
summary.what: Added Claude Code and Pi adapter tests that mix malformed JSONL lines, unsupported future records, accepted records, and tool/attachment-like content, then verify sync/find/read expose only accepted text.
summary.why: Issue #73 calls for malformed transcript fixtures and format-drift smoke tests so experimental source adapters have an explicit robustness contract under upstream raw-format drift.
summary.user_goal: Add malformed source adapter smoke fixtures for Sherlog

decisions.chose: Added coverage to the existing source adapter tests rather than changing parser implementation.
decisions.rationale: The current parsers already skip malformed JSON lines and unsupported records; this slice makes that contract explicit and regression-tested.

decisions.chose: Assert strict sync success, one indexed session, searchable accepted content, unreadable unsupported content, and clean read-page projections.
decisions.rationale: This proves robustness without broadening the adapter privacy surface.

rejected.alternative: Promote unsupported/future records into searchable projections
rejected.reason: That would violate #73 non-goals without a privacy design.

review_notes: Verified with npx vitest run src/sources/claude-code.test.ts src/sources/pi.test.ts, npm run check, and git diff --check -- src/sources/claude-code.test.ts src/sources/pi.test.ts.
Archive.zip remains an unrelated untracked local file and was not included in the commit.

architectural_claims: Experimental source adapters should tolerate malformed JSONL and unknown future records without leaking unsupported content.
behavioral_changes: Claude Code adapter tests now prove malformed lines and unsupported system records are skipped during sync while accepted messages remain searchable and readable.
Pi adapter tests now prove malformed lines, unknown future events, and toolCall content are skipped while accepted messages and compaction summaries remain searchable as intended.
security_implications: No production parser policy changed; tests lock in the existing privacy-preserving behavior for unsupported/tool-like records.
